title: Terminology coding to NCIt/HGNC/RxNorm/LOINC/ICD-O-3/HPO
context: Structured assertions are coded to open vocabularies first (NCIt, HGNC, RxNorm, LOINC, ICD-O-3, HPO) to avoid SNOMED licensing friction, with SNOMED CT attached only as a non-redistributed secondary mapping for licensed consumers. Performance status is coded to the age-appropriate scale. Codes must validate against the named vocabulary version.
objective: Build a terminology-coding stage that maps eligibility concepts to open vocabularies first, codes performance status to the age-appropriate scale, and attaches SNOMED CT only as a secondary mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Concepts map to open vocabularies first: NCIt, HGNC, RxNorm, LOINC, ICD-O-3, HPO.
Performance status coded to the age-appropriate scale (Lansky <16; Karnofsky/ECOG >=16) with scale recorded.
SNOMED CT attached only as a secondary mapping and not redistributed where prohibited.
Each code is validated to exist in the named vocabulary version; failures are flagged.
resources: NCIt, HGNC, RxNorm, LOINC, ICD-O-3, HPO documentation
